question.text: Mr. Gupta receives Rs. 1,500 as semi-annual interest on his Sovereign Gold Bond investment. What is the TDS treatment on this interest income?

answer.text: TDS at 10% is deducted if annual interest exceeds Rs. 5,000
TDS at 10% is deducted at source on each payment
No TDS is applicable on SGB interest, but the interest is taxable at the investor's slab rate
No TDS is applicable, and the interest is also fully exempt from income tax

explanation.text: **The Logic:** TDS is NOT applicable on interest earned from Sovereign Gold Bonds. However, this does not mean the interest is tax-free. The interest is taxable as "Income from Other Sources" at the investor's applicable income tax slab rate. The investor must declare this interest income while filing returns. **The Trap:** Option D is the most dangerous trap - no TDS does NOT mean no tax. Many investors (and even some advisers) confuse "no TDS" with "tax-free." The interest is definitely taxable; only the mechanism of advance collection (TDS) is absent. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A and Series X-A cover TDS provisions on various investment products. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always remind SGB investors to declare their interest income in tax returns even though no TDS is deducted. Failure to report can lead to notices from the tax department during assessment.
